summary: Reference matrix for exceptions, GC interaction, native callback pressure, and long-running stress cases.

cases.upstream_intent: Structured exception flow needs a happy-path baseline that includes finally behavior.
cases.zr_contract: Throw, catch, and finally follow one explicit unwind contract.
cases.expected_outcome: throw or catch or finally flow executes with stable control transfer
assertions: runtime unwind visits finally exactly once
exception object shape is stable
project fixture observes the expected return or error

return: throw or catch or finally flow executes with stable control transfer

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Exceptions should carry structured metadata instead of only raw strings.
cases.zr_contract: Exception values retain structured fields such as type, message, and source location.
cases.expected_outcome: raised errors expose stable structured fields
assertions: runtime exception object includes expected fields
artifact preserves structured error metadata where relevant
project fixture can inspect the structured error

exception: raised errors expose stable structured fields

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Unhandled exceptions need a stable externally visible contract.
cases.zr_contract: Unhandled exceptions surface as structured runtime failures without corrupting thread state.
cases.expected_outcome: unhandled exception leaves a stable error report and resettable runtime state
assertions: runtime prints or returns a structured unhandled error
thread state resets predictably after reporting
stress repeated unhandled errors do not corrupt future calls

exception: unhandled exception leaves a stable error report and resettable runtime state

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Native callback re-entry around exceptions is a high-risk runtime path.
cases.zr_contract: Native callback re-entry preserves exception ownership and unwinding invariants.
cases.expected_outcome: invalid or unsafe native callback re-entry fails deterministically
assertions: runtime reports the re-entry failure explicitly
native handle ownership remains valid after failure
stress repeated failures stay deterministic

exception: invalid or unsafe native callback re-entry fails deterministically

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Deep stacks and recursion expose exception-unwind limits.
cases.zr_contract: Deep exception stacks either unwind successfully or fail with a stable stack-depth diagnostic.
cases.expected_outcome: deep exception stacks keep deterministic unwind behavior
assertions: stack-depth limit or successful unwind is explicit
runtime does not corrupt call frames
stress deep recursion remains deterministic

return: deep exception stacks keep deterministic unwind behavior

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Long-running loops should not destabilize exception or GC contracts.
cases.zr_contract: Long loops preserve exception and GC invariants across repeated iterations.
cases.expected_outcome: long loop execution remains stable under periodic checks or exceptions
assertions: loop count or checkpoint behavior is stable
GC pressure does not corrupt live values
stress run completes without runtime corruption

return: long loop execution remains stable under periodic checks or exceptions

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Throwing through cleanup scopes needs explicit lifetime validation.
cases.zr_contract: Throws crossing cleanup scopes run cleanup deterministically before propagating the exception.
cases.expected_outcome: cleanup executes before exception propagation completes
assertions: cleanup count and order are asserted
exception propagation still reaches the handler or boundary
project fixture exposes the same cleanup order

return: cleanup executes before exception propagation completes
cleanup_trace: cleanup count and order are asserted
project fixture exposes the same cleanup order

cases.upstream_intent: GC while native or owned objects are live is a high-risk pressure path.
cases.zr_contract: GC respects active owned or native object liveness and finalization contracts during exception-capable execution.
cases.expected_outcome: GC during live owned or native object use preserves liveness and cleanup invariants
assertions: live handles remain reachable through GC
cleanup does not run early
stress repeated GC with active objects stays stable

return: GC during live owned or native object use preserves liveness and cleanup invariants

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Repeated import or unload cycles can regress exception and GC invariants together.
cases.zr_contract: Repeated import or unload under exception pressure preserves module and handle integrity.
cases.expected_outcome: repeated import or unload cycles do not leak exception state or native handles
assertions: module cache remains coherent
error state resets between cycles
stress run does not leak handles

return: repeated import or unload cycles do not leak exception state or native handles

cases.upstream_intent: Repeated throw and catch loops often uncover hidden state leaks.
cases.zr_contract: Repeated exception cycles preserve handler state, stack state, and object cleanup.
cases.expected_outcome: repeated throw or catch loops remain stable
assertions: handler stack depth returns to baseline
exception objects are not retained unexpectedly
stress repetition produces consistent results

return: repeated throw or catch loops remain stable

cases.upstream_intent: Finalization should not depend on magic method names in VM core.
cases.zr_contract: Finalizer and cleanup participation come from explicit contracts, not name-based runtime checks.
cases.expected_outcome: cleanup is not enabled by matching a magic finalizer name alone
assertions: runtime registers cleanup through explicit metadata
artifact preserves cleanup metadata instead of magic names
semantic checks do not branch on type names

return: cleanup is not enabled by matching a magic finalizer name alone
cleanup_trace: runtime registers cleanup through explicit metadata
artifact preserves cleanup metadata instead of magic names

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Reference runtimes sometimes permit host-specific swallowing paths that ZR should reject.
cases.zr_contract: ZR does not silently swallow runtime exceptions in shared execution paths without an explicit handler contract.
cases.expected_outcome: exceptions remain visible unless a documented handler consumes them
assertions: runtime reports unhandled exceptions explicitly
project fixture sees the same propagation behavior
artifact or diagnostics preserve handler metadata

return: exceptions remain visible unless a documented handler consumes them
